subject: Biology
title: How Viruses Infect
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Viruses Infect.

hook.text: A virus latches onto a cell like a key fitting a lock, using surface proteins to bind receptors.

buildup.text: Once inside, it injects its genetic material and takes over the cell's protein-making machinery.

discovery.text: The cell churns out hundreds of new virus copies until it bursts open, releasing them all.

twist.text: Some viruses hide in your DNA for years—herpes can reactivate decades after the first infection.

climax.text: Each virus targets specific cell types. HIV attacks immune cells. Rabies targets nerve cells.

quiz.question: How do some viruses persist in the body for years?
options: They hide in the host's DNA
